sourceMovie.title: Out of Africa
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Out of Africa (1985) as a history theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The history and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Duchess
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Out of Africa clicked for you, The Duchess (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in history and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Out of Africa?
faq.answer: Try The Duchess, The Edge of Love, Legends of the Fall—each shares DNA with Out of Africa while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Out of Africa?
faq.answer: Out of Africa (1985) is best described as History, Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Out of Africa worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
